root_text.an10.95:1.1: Atha kho uttiyo paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.95:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho uttiyo paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.95:1.3: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti?
root_text.an10.95:2.1: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti?
root_text.an10.95:3.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, antavā loko …pe…
root_text.an10.95:3.7: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā …
root_text.an10.95:3.8: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan”ti?
root_text.an10.95:3.10: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’”ti.
root_text.an10.95:4.1: “‘Kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno ‘abyākataṁ kho etaṁ, uttiya, mayā—
root_text.an10.95:4.2: sassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.95:5.1: ‘Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an10.95:5.2: ‘etampi kho, uttiya, abyākataṁ mayā asassato loko, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.95:6.1: ‘Kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, antavā loko …pe…
root_text.an10.95:6.7: hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā …
root_text.an10.95:6.8: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an10.95:6.10: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā, idameva saccaṁ moghamaññan’ti vadesi.
root_text.an10.95:7.1: “Abhiññāya kho ahaṁ, uttiya, sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desemi sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.an10.95:8.1: “Yaṁ panetaṁ bhavaṁ gotamo abhiññāya sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desesi sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya, sabbo vā tena loko nīyati upaḍḍho vā tibhāgo vā”ti?
root_text.an10.95:9.2: “mā hevaṁ kho uttiyo paribbājako pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ paṭilabhi:
root_text.an10.95:9.3: ‘sabbasāmukkaṁsikaṁ vata me samaṇo gotamo pañhaṁ puṭṭho saṁsādeti, no vissajjeti, na nūna visahatī’ti.
root_text.an10.95:9.4: Tadassa uttiyassa paribbājakassa dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāyā”ti.
root_text.an10.95:10.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando uttiyaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.95:10.3: Upamāya m’idhekacce viññū purisā bhāsitassa atthaṁ ājānanti.
root_text.an10.95:10.4: Seyyathāpi, āvuso uttiya, rañño paccantimaṁ nagaraṁ daḷhuddhāpaṁ daḷhapākāratoraṇaṁ ekadvāraṁ.
root_text.an10.95:10.5: Tatrassa dovāriko paṇḍito byatto medhāvī aññātānaṁ nivāretā ñātānaṁ pavesetā.
root_text.an10.95:10.6: So tassa nagarassa samantā anupariyāyapathaṁ anukkamati. Anupariyāyapathaṁ anukkamamāno na passeyya pākārasandhiṁ vā pākāravivaraṁ vā, antamaso biḷāranikkhamanamattampi.
root_text.an10.95:10.7: No ca khvassa evaṁ ñāṇaṁ hoti: ‘ettakā pāṇā imaṁ nagaraṁ pavisanti vā nikkhamanti vā’ti.
root_text.an10.95:10.8: Atha khvassa evamettha hoti: ‘ye kho keci oḷārikā pāṇā imaṁ nagaraṁ pavisanti vā nikkhamanti vā, sabbe te iminā dvārena pavisanti vā nikkhamanti vā’ti.
root_text.an10.95:11.1: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso uttiya, na tathāgatassa evaṁ ussukkaṁ hoti: ‘sabbo vā tena loko nīyati, upaḍḍho vā, tibhāgo vā’ti.
root_text.an10.95:11.2: Atha kho evamettha tathāgatassa hoti: ‘ye kho keci lokamhā nīyiṁsu vā nīyanti vā nīyissanti vā, sabbe te pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe, catūsu satipaṭṭhānesu suppatiṭṭhitacittā, satta bojjhaṅge yathābhūtaṁ bhāvetvā.
root_text.an10.95:11.3: Evamete lokamhā nīyiṁsu vā nīyanti vā nīyissanti vā’ti.
root_text.an10.95:11.4: Yadeva kho tvaṁ, āvuso uttiya, bhagavantaṁ pañhaṁ apucchi tadevetaṁ pañhaṁ bhagavantaṁ aññena pariyāyena apucchi.
root_text.an10.95:11.5: Tasmā te taṁ bhagavā na byākāsī”ti.

translation_text.an10.95:1.1: Then the wanderer Uttiya went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an10.95:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an10.95:1.3: “Worthy Gotama, is this right: ‘The cosmos is eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.95:1.4: “This has not been declared by me, Uttiya.”
translation_text.an10.95:2.1: “Then is this right: ‘The cosmos is not eternal. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.95:2.2: “This has not been declared by me, Uttiya.”
translation_text.an10.95:3.1: “Then is this right: ‘The cosmos is finite …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.3: ‘The soul and the body are one and the same …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.4: ‘The soul is one thing, the body another …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.5: ‘A realized one still exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.6: ‘A realized one no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.7: ‘A realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death …’ …
translation_text.an10.95:3.8: ‘A Realized One neither exists nor doesn’t exist after death. This is the only truth, anything else is futile’?”
translation_text.an10.95:3.9: “This has not been declared by me, Uttiya.”
translation_text.an10.95:4.1: “When asked about all these points, worthy Gotama says that they have not been declared by him.
translation_text.an10.95:6.11: So what then has been declared by Mister Gotama?”
translation_text.an10.95:7.1: “Uttiya, I teach my disciples from my own insight in order to purify sentient beings, to get past sorrow and crying, to make an end of pain and sadness, to end the cycle of suffering, and to realize extinguishment.”
translation_text.an10.95:8.1: “But when the worthy Gotama teaches in this way, is the whole world saved, or half, or a third?”
translation_text.an10.95:8.2: But when he said this, the Buddha kept silent.
translation_text.an10.95:9.2: “The wanderer Uttiya must not get the harmful misconception:
translation_text.an10.95:9.3: ‘When the ascetic Gotama was asked this all-important question he falters without answering. He just can’t do it!’
translation_text.an10.95:9.4: That would be for his lasting harm and suffering.”
translation_text.an10.95:10.1: Then Ānanda said to the wanderer Uttiya,
translation_text.an10.95:10.2: “Well then, Reverend Uttiya, I shall give you a simile.
translation_text.an10.95:10.3: For by means of a simile some sensible people understand the meaning of what is said.
translation_text.an10.95:10.4: Suppose there was a king’s frontier citadel with fortified embankments, ramparts, and arches, and a single gate.
translation_text.an10.95:10.5: And it has a gatekeeper who is astute, competent, and clever. He keeps strangers out and lets known people in.
translation_text.an10.95:10.6: As he walks around the patrol path, he doesn’t see a hole or cleft in the wall, not even one big enough for a cat to slip out.
translation_text.an10.95:10.7: He doesn’t know how many creatures enter or leave the citadel.
translation_text.an10.95:10.8: But he does know that whatever sizable creatures enter or leave the citadel, all of them do so via this gate.
translation_text.an10.95:11.1: In the same way, it’s not the Realized One’s concern whether the whole world is saved by this, or half, or a third.
translation_text.an10.95:11.2: But the Realized One knows that whoever is saved from the world—whether in the past, the future, or the present—all have given up the five hindrances, corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom. They have firmly established their mind in the four kinds of mindfulness meditation. And they have truly developed the seven awakening factors.
translation_text.an10.95:11.3: That’s how they’re saved from the world, in the past, future, or present.
translation_text.an10.95:11.4: Uttiya, you were just asking the Buddha the same question as before in a different way.

variant_text.an10.95:8.1: sabbo vā → sabbo ca (pts1ed, mr) | nīyati → nīyissati (bj); niyyāssati (sya-all); niyyissati (pts1ed) | tibhāgo vā”ti → tibhāgo vā (sya-all); tibhāgo vāti vadehi (mr)
variant_text.an10.95:11.4: Yadeva kho tvaṁ → yadeva khvettha (mr) | pañhaṁ → imaṁ pañhaṁ (sya-all, mr)
